untranslatable_reason: Signal requires calculation of spread GPM (Gross Processing Margin) across three correlated commodity contracts (3× crude oil long vs 2× gasoline + 1× heating oil short) with transaction cost deduction. This multi-leg spread arbitrage condition cannot be expressed using single-instrument price/technical indicators. Requires custom spread pricing logic, inter-commodity correlation analysis, and carrying cost models not available in the standard indicator set. The 'minimum_gpm' threshold is marked as [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] in source parameters, indicating it was not numerically defined.
translation_notes: KAUFMAN_DRY_13 is a sophisticated crack-spread trade (crude-to-refined-products arbitrage). Entry requires: (1) Computing weighted spread: 3×crude_price - 2×gasoline_price - 1×heating_oil_price; (2) Deducting execution slippage, commissions, and storage/carrying costs; (3) Comparing net result to minimum profitable GPM threshold. Exit condition 'AUTHOR_SILENT' suggests holding until reversal or market-driven exit. A backtest engine would need custom spread calculation modules and multi-contract position management not supported by standard single-price indicators. Historical ratio validation from CRB Commodity Yearbook should inform position sizing and threshold calibration.
